claim_text: Patient fell on the ice and experienced right ankle pain and swelling.
Tenderness to palpation is present on the right ankle.
No laxity on anterior drawer and inversion is noted on the right ankle.
Capillary refill is <3 seconds on the right foot.
Strong dorsalis pedis pulse is present on the right foot.
Intact sensation to light touch is present on the right foot.
Patient has a right ankle sprain.
Patient should keep leg elevated, especially when seated.
Patient should continue to ice the ankle.
Patient should use an Aircast for ankle immobilization.
Patient should use crutches for the next 1-2 days.
Patient should follow up in 2 weeks to reassess.
Patient should return to clinic if symptoms arise before the follow-up appointment.
